input_text: At the time of the first iteration of the American Association of Clinical Endocrinology algorithm for glycemic control, thiazolidinediones, alpha-glucosidase inhibitors, metformin, and sulfonylureas/glinides were in use.
The first iteration of the American Association of Clinical Endocrinology algorithm for glycemic control also included the addition of exenatide and dipeptidyl peptidase-4 inhibitors.

input_text: The American Association of Clinical Endocrinology published the Comprehensive Type 2 Diabetes Management Algorithm in 2013, which incorporated new sections on the management of overweight/obesity, dyslipidemia, and hypertension.

input_text: The 2023 American Association of Clinical Endocrinology algorithm continues to emphasize lifestyle modification and treatment of overweight/obesity as key pillars of the management of prediabetes and diabetes mellitus.
The 2023 American Association of Clinical Endocrinology algorithm highlights the importance of appropriate management of the atherosclerotic risk factors of dyslipidemia and hypertension.

input_text: The 2023 American Association of Clinical Endocrinology algorithm includes a complication-centric approach, beyond glucose levels, to frame decisions regarding first-line pharmacologic choices for the treatment of persons with diabetes mellitus.

input_text: The American Association of Clinical Endocrinology task force members/authors acknowledge that health care disparities and lack of access to newer medications remain a significant barrier for some persons with diabetes mellitus.
The 2023 American Association of Clinical Endocrinology algorithm is divided into discrete graphic sections that outline the principles for management of Type 2 Diabetes.

input_text: The 2023 American Association of Clinical Endocrinology algorithm guides management of adiposity-based chronic disease, prediabetes, and atherosclerotic risk factors of dyslipidemia and hypertension.
The 2023 American Association of Clinical Endocrinology algorithm includes direction for insulin initiation and titration.

input_text: Weight loss improves many of the cardiometabolic and biomechanical components of adiposity-based chronic disease, including increased glycemia, dyslipidemia, elevated blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, nonalcoholic fatty liver disease, sleep apnea, and osteoarthritis.
The American Association of Endocrinology Comprehensive Type 2 Diabetes Management Algorithm is based on principles that underlie all therapy for Type 2 Diabetes.

input_text: Lifestyle modification for Type 2 Diabetes includes exercise, healthy dietary changes, smoking cessation, and reduced alcohol intake.
Lifestyle modification for Type 2 Diabetes also includes assessment and management of sleep disorders and depression.

input_text: The choice of antihyperglycemic therapy for Type 2 Diabetes reflects glycemic targets, atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease, congestive heart failure, chronic kidney disease, overweight/obesity, and nonalcoholic fatty liver disease.
Glycemic control has an essential role in the prevention and decreased progression of Type 2 Diabetes complications.

input_text: The 2022 American Association of Endocrinology Clinical Practice Guidelines update recommends that clinicians prescribe a glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist or a sodium glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitor for Type 2 Diabetes patients with established or high risk for atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease, heart failure, and/or chronic kidney disease.
The choice of antihyperglycemic therapy for Type 2 Diabetes includes consideration of the potential benefit for stroke or nonalcoholic fatty liver disease.
